description_long: This is a large, four-engine propeller-driven airplane with a high-mounted straight wing configuration. The fuselage is elongated and cylindrical, with a forward-positioned, enclosed cockpit featuring multiple windows. The wings are equipped with four turboprop engines, each with multi-blade propellers, and extend symmetrically from the upper section of the fuselage. The tail section features a prominent T-tail design, with a horizontal stabilizer mounted atop a single vertical fin. The exterior is primarily light gray with subtle blue and red markings on the tail, and the landing gear is not visible in these views. The overall design suggests a cargo or military transport aircraft, built for robustness and capacity.
description: A large, high-wing, four-engine turboprop airplane with a T-tail and forward enclosed cockpit, primarily light gray with blue and red tail markings.
description_view_0: Side view showing the elongated fuselage, high-mounted straight wings with four turboprop engines, and the T-tail configuration.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the wide wingspan, four propellers, and the forward cockpit windows.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, with clear visibility of the engines and T-tail.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the T-tail structure, full wingspan, and the alignment of the engines and propellers.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Four turboprop engines mounted on the wings.
attribute_tail_design: T-tail configuration with a single vertical stabilizer and a high-mounted horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible in the provided views.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows.
